sections.paragraph:
m. Arab. مَشَلُ, Syr. ܡܰܬܠܳ [Ch. מַתְלָא]

(1) similitude, parable, Eze 17:2 24:3 .

(2) sentence, opinion, γνώμη, sententious saying, (such as consists in the ingenious comparison of two things or opinions, compare Pro 26:1, Pro 26:2, Pro 26:3, Pro 26:6, Pro 26:7, Pro 26:8, Pro 26:9, 11 Pro 26:11, 14 Pro 26:14, 17 Pro 26:17, chap. 25 , seq.), Pro 1:1, 10:1 25:1 26:7 , Pro 26:9 Ecc 12:9 Job 13:12 1Ki 5:12. Sentences of this kind often pass into proverbs ( 1Sa 24:13); hence מָשָׁל is-

(3) a proverb, παροιμία, e.g. 1Sa 10:12 Eze 12:2, Eze 12:3 12:22 , 23 Eze 12:23(compare παραβολή, Lu. 4:13 ).

(4) generally a song, poem, the particular verses of which (by what are called the laws of parallelism) commonly consist of two hemistichs of similar argument and form; specially used of a prophecy, Num 23:7, 18 Num 23:18of a discourse or a didactic poem, Job 27:1 29:1 Psa 49:5 78:2 often used of a derisive poem, Isa 14:4 Mic 2:4 Hab 2:6. הָיָה לְמָשָׁל וְלִשְׁנִינָה to become a by-word, Deu 28:37 1Ki 9:7. (Arabic مَشَلُ parable, fable, sentence; plur. أمْثَالُ verses.)

משל

[Mashal], pr.n.; see מִשְׁאָל .
